raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1054 will ensure that all public high school students in California have access to computer science education.",
    "California has fallen behind 27 other states in prioritizing access to computer science education.",
    "Ensuring that every California high school student has access to computer science courses will help close the gender and diversity gap in tech fields.",
    "Every student deserves to be equipped with the knowledge and skills necessary to participate in modern society.",
    "Too many students, particularly Black, Latinx, Indigenous, and low-income students, do not have access to foundational learning opportunities in computer science.",
    "Requiring all high schools to offer at least one computer science course is essential for preparing the next generation for career pathways.",
    "Computer science education allows students to develop critical thinking and problem-solving skills.",
    "75.1% of high school students in California attend a school that offers computer science, but significant disparities in access persist.",
    "Students from historically excluded racial and ethnic groups are less likely to attend schools that offer computer science education.",
    "Rural schools, urban schools, and schools with high percentages of economically disadvantaged students are less likely to offer computer science courses."
  ]
}
```
